name: Editor AI
description: Generate Geometry Dash levels using AI!

ai-provider.name: AI Provider
ai-provider.description: Which backend to call. Configure the matching section below.

difficulty.name: Difficulty
difficulty.description: Target difficulty for the AI. Canned values: easy / medium / hard / extreme. Free-form text is also accepted (e.g. 'demon', 'tutorial').

style.name: Style
style.description: Visual / gameplay style. Canned values: modern / retro / flow / memory. Free-form text is also accepted.

length.name: Length
length.description: Approximate level length. Canned values: short / medium / long / xl / xxl. Free-form text is also accepted.

max-objects.name: Max Objects
max-objects.description: Upper bound on objects the AI may emit per generation.

target-object-count.name: Target Objects
target-object-count.description: Minimum total objects a generation must reach. The AI is asked to continue until it gets there (or extension rounds run out). <cg>0 = off</c>. Capped by Max Objects.

edit-target-ops.name: Edit Workload Target
edit-target-ops.description: When the AI reworks an existing level, it must total at least this many edits (objects moved + deleted + added + restyled) before the result stages — it is asked to continue until it gets there. Small follow-up tweaks (under 30 edits) stage immediately. <cg>0 = off</c>.

spawn-batch-size.name: Spawn Speed
spawn-batch-size.description: Objects placed per editor tick during preview. Higher = faster but jankier.

example-level-ids.name: Example Level IDs
example-level-ids.description: Up to 5 comma-separated GD level IDs the AI downloads as style references at the start of every generation (requires AI tools). Leave empty for none.

ai-ground-y.name: Ground Y
ai-ground-y.description: Editor Y of the visible ground line. 105 is default. Lower if blocks float; higher if they bury.

gemini-model.name: Gemini Model
gemini-model.description: Use <cg>Fetch model list</c> in the in-mod Provider tab to pull the live list, or type any Gemini model id.

gemini-api-key.name: Gemini API Key
gemini-api-key.description: From Google AI Studio. Stays on this device in Geode save data.

claude-model.name: Claude Model
claude-model.description: Use <cg>Fetch model list</c> in the in-mod Provider tab to pull the live list, or type any Claude model id.

claude-api-key.name: Claude API Key
claude-api-key.description: From console.anthropic.com. Stays on this device.

openai-model.name: OpenAI Model
openai-model.description: Use <cg>Fetch model list</c> in the in-mod Provider tab to pull the live list, or type any OpenAI model id.

openai-api-key.name: OpenAI API Key
openai-api-key.description: From platform.openai.com. Stays on this device.

ministral-model.name: Mistral Model
ministral-model.description: Use <cg>Fetch model list</c> in the in-mod Provider tab to pull the live list, or type any Mistral model id.

ministral-api-key.name: Mistral API Key
ministral-api-key.description: From console.mistral.ai. Stays on this device.

huggingface-api-key.name: HF Token
huggingface-api-key.description: From huggingface.co/settings/tokens. Stays on this device.

openrouter-model.name: OpenRouter Model
openrouter-model.description: Provider/model slug, e.g. google/gemini-2.5-flash or anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet.

openrouter-api-key.name: OpenRouter API Key
openrouter-api-key.description: From openrouter.ai/keys. One key, many hosted models.

deepseek-model.name: DeepSeek Model
deepseek-model.description: Use <cg>Fetch model list</c> in the in-mod Provider tab to pull the live list, or type any DeepSeek model id.

use-platinum.name: Use Platinum
use-platinum.description: Route Ollama requests through the EditorAI Platinum cloud proxy instead of localhost.

ollama-model.name: Ollama Model Tag
ollama-model.description: Tag as shown by `ollama list`. e.g. entity12208/editorai:v3-7b.

ollama-timeout.name: Ollama Timeout (s)
ollama-timeout.description: How long to wait for a local Ollama response before giving up.

lm-studio-model.name: LM Studio Model
lm-studio-model.description: Loaded model name in LM Studio. Start the server before generating.

llama-cpp-model.name: llama.cpp Model
llama-cpp-model.description: Model name (often 'default' unless your server expects something specific).

custom-provider-name.name: Display Name
custom-provider-name.description: Cosmetic only. Shown in the AI popup info panel.

custom-provider-url.name: Endpoint URL
custom-provider-url.description: Base URL or full /chat/completions URL. OpenAI-compatible bodies are used.

custom-provider-api-key.name: API Key
custom-provider-api-key.description: Leave blank if your server needs no auth.

custom-provider-model.name: Model
custom-provider-model.description: Model name expected by your endpoint.

custom-provider-auth.name: Auth Header Template
custom-provider-auth.description: ${KEY} is replaced with your API key. Clear to send no header.

bypass-char-filter.name: Character Filter Bypass
bypass-char-filter.description: Let every text box in the game accept any printable character (same behavior as Eclipse Menu's bypass). Off = vanilla filtering.

bypass-char-limit.name: Character Limit Bypass
bypass-char-limit.description: Remove the maximum text length on every text box in the game (same behavior as Eclipse Menu's bypass). Off = vanilla limits.

ui-anim-speed.name: UI Animation Speed
ui-anim-speed.description: How fast the EditorAI panel opens and closes. <cg>1</c> = slow fade (2 seconds), <cg>10</c> = instant.

enable-ai-tools.name: Enable AI Tools
enable-ai-tools.description: Let the AI call helper tools (web search, level analysis, song lookup, etc.) before composing.

enable-advanced-features.name: Triggers & Colors
enable-advanced-features.description: Allow the AI to emit color/move/pulse triggers and group IDs.

enable-rate-limiting.name: Rate Limiting
enable-rate-limiting.description: Throttle generations. Leave on for hosted APIs to avoid quota burns.

rate-limit-seconds.name: Rate Limit (s)
rate-limit-seconds.description: Minimum seconds between generations when rate limiting is on.

show-ai-output.name: Save AI Output
show-ai-output.description: Write each generation's full raw AI response to last-ai-response.txt in this mod's save folder. Off by default.

enable-rating.name: Rate Generations
enable-rating.description: Show the thumbs up/down rating prompt after generation.

max-feedback-examples.name: Feedback Examples
max-feedback-examples.description: How many past rated generations to use as few-shot context.

refinement-rounds.name: Refinement Rounds
refinement-rounds.description: How many self-review passes the AI runs after the initial draft. 0 disables refinement.

enable-self-critique.name: Final Self-Check
enable-self-critique.description: One extra round after refinements where the AI scores its own level and patches the weakest area (or confirms it's good). Costs at most one additional API call.

enable-vision.name: Vision (level snapshots)
enable-vision.description: When the model supports images (Claude, Gemini, GPT-4o, LLaVA...), attach a rendered snapshot of the level to review and follow-up turns so the AI can SEE what it built.

two-pass-generation.name: Two-Pass Generation
two-pass-generation.description: Generate the gameplay skeleton first; after you accept it, a second pass adds decoration, color triggers, and atmosphere without touching gameplay.

subagent-provider.name: Subagent Provider
subagent-provider.description: Optional second model the main AI can consult via the ask_subagent tool (e.g. a big cloud model asking your local Ollama for object suggestions, or vice versa). Empty disables the tool.

subagent-model.name: Subagent Model
subagent-model.description: Model name for the subagent provider. Empty uses that provider's configured model.

copilot-mode.name: Copilot Mode
copilot-mode.description: While you edit, EditorAI watches for death zones and physics problems and automatically proposes a fix blueprint (you still accept or deny). At most one auto-fix every 2 minutes.

allow-telemetry.name: Auto-Share Generations (Telemetry)
allow-telemetry.description: Opt-in. While ON, <cy>every generation</c> is uploaded automatically to the community training collector: prompt, settings, generated objects, and ratings (yours and the AI's self-review). Never your identity, never API keys. Levels rated above 5 become training data for the free community models.

about:
Generate Geometry Dash levels by describing them in plain text.

<cy>How to use</c>
Press <cg>E</c> anywhere (rebindable in Settings; on mobile, tap the floating <cg>AI</c> bubble) to open the EditorAI panel.
- <cy>Chat</c>: hit <cg>+ new chat</c>, pick a target - the current editor, a brand-new level, or any local level - describe what you want, and Generate. Each generation is a conversation: watch it think and work, then keep talking to it for as long as you like - small edits, big reworks, plans, questions. Conversations survive restarts and pick up where they left off.
- <cy>Settings</c>: choose among 12 AI providers (Gemini, Claude, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Mistral, HuggingFace, DeepSeek, Ollama/Platinum, LM Studio, llama.cpp, custom, and Manual - copy-paste with any chatbot, no key), paste your API key, and tune generation.

Generated objects appear on their own editor layer - the rest of the level fades back - and you accept, edit, or deny the result. Edits the AI makes to existing objects are previewed the same way and fully reversible until you accept.
